target: data/wins.json WIN-067 — one-sentence targeted patch to detail_evidence. Replace the closing sentence of the WGS84-tautology paragraph (currently 'fails by 170×') with a 170–1,430× disambiguation that names the two comparison types and points back to the opening paragraph. Resolves ISS-1397 (c7/c8 carry-forward H1 major).
patch_instructions: One edit to the WIN with id='067' in data/wins.json. In detail_evidence, find the unique substring detail_evidence_sentence_patch.before (verified 1 occurrence on 2026-04-22) and replace with detail_evidence_sentence_patch.after. No other WIN-067 fields are touched. All other paragraphs (opening block, Domain 4 / WGS84-tautology paragraph head, Mascon paragraph, refraction-cascade paragraph) are unchanged. The verdict ('Self-Contradicted') and finding are unchanged.

detail_evidence_sentence_patch.before: The formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×.
detail_evidence_sentence_patch.after: The formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on the comparison — 170× is the dimensionless-fractional lower bound (90% predicted variation vs 0.53% observed) computed on different baselines and deliberately understates the discrepancy, while 1,430× is the apples-to-apples absolute-m/s² miss at Antarctic latitude (see the opening paragraph).
detail_evidence_sentence_patch.rationale: c8 hole #1 recommendation, applied verbatim in form A with added one-clause gloss naming what each ratio measures. The existing lead paragraph already lays out both numbers ('1,430×' for apples-to-apples m/s² and '170×' as 'a ratio of dimensionless percentages computed on different baselines, which understates the true discrepancy'). The closing sentence of the WGS84-tautology paragraph formerly reverted to 170× without qualifier, which a hostile reader could quote as inconsistent with the 1,430× headline. The new sentence closes the asymmetry without changing the verdict or the underlying arguments, and explicitly routes the reader back to the opening paragraph for the derivation of each number.

holes_addressed.hole: H1 (major) — ISS-1397 / c7 hole #1 / c8 hole #1 — 1,430× vs 170× asymmetry unresolved across 3 cycles
holes_addressed.description: The lead paragraph of detail_evidence computes the apples-to-apples error as 1,430× (8.87 m/s² predicted drop ÷ 0.0062 m/s² observed Antarctic drop) and offers 170× as a conservative dimensionless lower bound (90%/0.53% on different baselines, flagged as understating the true discrepancy). The closing sentence of the Domain 4 / WGS84-tautology paragraph then says 'fails by 170×' without the lower-bound qualifier and without pointing to the 1,430× apples-to-apples framing two paragraphs up. A reader encountering only the second number, or greping for 'fails by', gets two apparently-contradictory answers (differing by a factor of 8) to the question 'how badly does the dome's geometry fail?' EXP-215 explicitly scoped around this line (its patch_instructions #5 said 'do not change the ~1,430× headline that follows'), and EXP-221 addressed the Domain 4 falsification-violation elevation and the Mascon r=0.92 rebuttal but did not touch this sentence. Persisted across c7 and c8 curmudgeon cycles.
holes_addressed.fix: Replace the closing sentence 'The formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×.' with the same opening clause plus a 170–1,430× disambiguation that explicitly names (a) 170× as the dimensionless-fractional lower bound computed on different baselines (understating the discrepancy) and (b) 1,430× as the apples-to-apples absolute-m/s² miss at Antarctic latitude, with a pointer back to the opening paragraph for the derivation. Form follows c8 hole #1 recommendation option A with a short gloss on what each ratio measures. Verdict and other arguments are untouched.
holes_addressed.inverts: Before: single 170× figure at the closing, reading as inconsistent with the 1,430× headline. After: explicit range 170–1,430× with the comparison type attached to each endpoint, so a reader lands on the same number regardless of where they start reading.

new_evidence_added: No new evidence — the 170× and 1,430× figures and their derivations already appear in the opening paragraph of detail_evidence. This patch only disambiguates them at the point where a careful reader could otherwise mistake them for contradictory.

anticipated_objections.objection: The opening paragraph already gives the 170–1,430× range and labels 170× as a 'conservative dimensionless lower bound.' Adding this gloss at the closing is redundant.
anticipated_objections.response: It is redundant by design. Curmudgeon cycles 7 and 8 both flagged this as a genuine snag for a reader who greps for 'fails by' or starts reading at the WGS84-tautology paragraph — the disambiguation lives only in paragraph 1 and the closing-line form defaults to 170×. A one-clause gloss at the second occurrence costs ~40 words and removes a debating handle a hostile reader can quote ('your own numbers disagree by a factor of 8'). The opening paragraph's framing is preserved verbatim.

anticipated_objections.objection: Naming both numbers at the closing invites a defender to argue we are cherry-picking whichever ratio sounds worse.
anticipated_objections.response: The gloss explicitly names what each number measures and which is the lower bound, and the opening paragraph already states '170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation).' A defender quoting the closing-only line now sees the same range the headline carries; no new framing is introduced. If anything, naming 170× as the understated bound makes the review less exposed to a cherry-pick accusation, not more.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not fix the arithmetic-precision slip (0.006 vs 0.0062) in the same patch?
anticipated_objections.response: The arithmetic precision slip is c7 hole #6 / c8 hole #2, severity minor, and is a separate logical unit (replace '~0.006 m/s²' with '0.0062 m/s²' in detail_evidence and tldr_evidence). It is not covered by ISS-1397. Bundling it here would widen the patch beyond the scope the decider assigned and make the substring anchors more fragile. If the decider wants the precision fix, it can be scoped as its own issue/EXP — it is a two-occurrence substring replacement and will not conflict with this patch.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's critic deserves a self-consistent failure magnitude across a single WIN. Our opening paragraph already lays out both comparison types and labels 170× as 'a ratio of dimensionless percentages computed on different baselines, which understates the true discrepancy' — that framing is correct and the kernel of truth for this patch is that the criticism itself is well-calibrated. The fix is not a new argument but a pointer back to the argument the opening paragraph already makes, placed where a reader would otherwise default to the narrower number.
out_of_scope_deliberately: c7 hole #6 / c8 hole #2 (minor) — arithmetic precision slip (~0.006 → 0.0062 m/s² in detail_evidence ×2 and tldr_evidence ×1). Not covered by ISS-1397; scope to its own fix.
c6 hole #5 / c8 hole #3 (minor) — Shepherd 2012 citation imprecision (DOI resolves to ice-mass-balance meta-analysis, not a PGIA-primary citation). Not covered by ISS-1397; scope to its own fix.
No changes to WIN-067 finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_claim, detail_extra, detail_verdict_text, verdict, verdict_history, code_analysis — all unchanged.
propagation_note: EXP-221 integrated two moderate c8 patches on 2026-04-22 (Domain 4 falsification-violation elevation + Mascon r=0.92 common-cause rebuttal). EXP-223 picks up the outstanding major c8 hole #1 the decider explicitly assigned as ISS-1397. After integration, WIN-067 carries no open major curmudgeon holes; the two remaining minor items (arithmetic precision + Shepherd citation) remain unresolved as documented in out_of_scope.
